name: aaa accounting identity
detail: Enable accounting and to create an accounting method list for Session Aware Networking subscriber services.
syntax: aaa accounting identity {method-list-name | default} start-stop [broadcast] group {server-group-name | radius | tacacs+} [group {server-group-name | radius | tacacs+}]

params.name: method-list-name
params.description: Name of the method list for which to create accounting services by specifying the accounting methods that follow this name.

params.name: default
params.description: Creates a default method list for accounting services using the accounting methods that follow this keyword.

params.name: start-stop
params.description: Sends a “start” accounting notice at the beginning of a process and a “stop” accounting notice at the end of a process. The “start” accounting record is sent in the background. The requested user process begins regardless of whether the “start” accounting notice was received by the accounting server.

params.name: broadcast
params.description: (Optional) Sends accounting records to multiple authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) servers. Simultaneously sends accounting records to the first server in each group. If the first server is unavailable, the device uses the backup servers defined within that group.

params.name: group
params.description: Specifies one or more server groups to use for accounting services. Server groups are applied in the specified order. server-group-name Named subset of RADIUS or TACACS+ servers as defined by the aaa group server radius command or aaa group server tacacs+ command.

params.name: radius
params.description: Uses the list of all RADIUS servers configured with the radius-server host command.

params.name: tacacs+
params.description: Uses the list of all TACACS+ servers configured with the tacacs-server host command.

usageSummary: The aaa accounting identity command enables accounting services and creates method lists that define specific accounting methods for Session Aware Networking subscriber services.  A method list identifies the list of security servers to which the network access server sends accounting records.  Cisco IOS software supports the following two methods of accounting for Session Aware Networking: Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.

name: aaa local authentication
detail: Specify the method lists to use for local authentication and authorization from a Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) server.
syntax: aaa local authentication {method-list-name | default} authorization {method-list-name | default}
noForm: no aaa local authentication {method-list-name | default} authorization {method-list-name | default}

params.name: default
params.description: Uses the default AAA method list.

usageSummary: Use the aaa local authentication command to retrieve Extensible Authentication Protocol (EAP) credentials from local or remote LDAP servers.  The following example shows how to configure local authentication to use the method list named EAP_LIST: aaa new-model aaa local authentication EAP_LIST authorization EAP_LIST Related Commands aaa Enables the AAA access control model.  new-model ldap server Defines an LDAP server.

name: absolute-timer
detail: Enable an absolute timeout for subscriber sessions.

params.name: minutes
params.description: Maximum session duration, in minutes. Range: 1 to 65535. Default: 0, which disables the timer.

usageSummary: Use the absolute-timer command to limit the number of minutes that a subscriber session can remain active.  After this timer expires, a session must repeat the process of establishing its connection as if it were a new request.

name: access-group
detail: Apply an access list to sessions using a service template.

params.name: access-list-name
params.description: Name of the access control list (ACL) to apply.

usageSummary: Use the access-group command to apply a locally configured ACL to sessions on which the service template is activated.

name: access-session closed
detail: Prevent preauthentication access on a port.
usageSummary: The access-session closed command closes access to a port, preventing clients or devices from gaining network access before authentication is performed.  The following example shows how to set port 1/0/2 to closed access.  interface GigabitEthernet 1/0/2 access-session host-mode single-host access-session closed access-session port-control auto access-session control-direction in Related Commands access-session control-direction Sets the direction of authentication control on a port.

name: access-session control-direction
detail: Set the direction of authentication control on a port.

params.name: in
params.description: Enables unidirectional control on the port.

usageSummary: Use the access-session control-direction command to set the port control to either unidirectional or bidirectional.  The in keyword configures a port as unidirectional, allowing a device on the network to “wake up” the client and force it to reauthenticate.  The port can send packets to the host but cannot receive packets from the host.

name: access-session host-mode
detail: Allow hosts to gain access to a controlled port.

params.name: multi-auth
params.description: Specifies that multiple clients can be authenticated on the port at any given time. This is the default value. multi-domain Specifies that only one client per domain (DATA or VOICE) can be authenticated at a time.

params.name: multi-host
params.description: Specifies that after the first client is authenticated all subsequent clients are allowed access.

params.name: single-host
params.description: Specifies that only one client can be authenticated on a port at any given time. A security violation occurs if more than one client is detected.

usageSummary: Before you use this command, you must enable the access-session port-control auto command.  In multi-host mode, only one of the attached hosts has to be successfully authorized for all hosts to be granted network access.  If the port becomes unauthorized (reauthentication fails or an Extensible Authentication Protocol over LAN (EAPOL) logoff message is received), all attached clients are denied access to the network.

name: access-session port-control
detail: Set the authorization state of a port.

params.name: auto
params.description: Enables port-based authentication and causes the port to begin in the unauthorized state, allowing only Extensible Authentication Protocol over LAN (EAPOL) frames to be sent and received through the port.

params.name: force-authorized
params.description: Disables IEEE 802.1X on the interface and causes the port to change to the authorized state without requiring any authentication exchange. The port transmits and receives normal traffic without 802.1X-based authentication of the client. This is the default value. force-unauthorized Denies all access through this interface by forcing the port to change to the unauthorized state, ignoring all attempts by the client to authenticate.

usageSummary: The authentication process begins when the link state of the port transitions from down to up or when an EAPOL-start frame is received.  The system requests the identity of the client and begins relaying authentication messages between the client and the authentication server.  The following example shows how to set the authorization state on port 1/0/2 to automatic: interface GigabitEthernet 1/0/2 access-session host-mode single-host access-session closed access-session port-control auto access-session control-direction in Related Commands access-session closed Prevents preauthentication access on a port.

name: access-session
detail: Configures access session information in an interface templates.
syntax: aceess-session {closed | control-direction | {all | in } | host-mode | {multi-auth | multi-domain | multi-host | single-host } | interface-template sticky | port-control | {auto | force-authorized | force-unauthorized}}
noForm: no aceess-session {closed | control-direction | host-mode | interface-template sticky | port-control}

params.name: closed
params.description: Enables closed access on ports. Closed access is disabled by default.

params.name: control-direction
params.description: Sets the traffic control direction on the interface.

params.name: all
params.description: Sets control for both inbound and outbound traffic.

params.name: in
params.description: Sets traffic control on both directions.

params.name: host-mode
params.description: Sets the host mode for authentication on the interface.

params.name: multi-auth
params.description: Sets multiple authentication mode as the host mode on the interface.

params.name: multi-domain
params.description: Sets multiple domain mode as the host mode on the interface.

params.name: multi-host
params.description: Sets multiple host mode as the host mode on the interface.

params.name: single-host
params.description: Sets single host mode as the host mode on the interface. interface-template sticky Sets the interface as sticky so that the interface template is retained even when the link is down or the device is disconnected.

params.name: port-control
params.description: Sets the port state.

params.name: auto
params.description: Sets the port state as automatic.

params.name: force-authorized
params.description: Sets the port state as authorized.

params.name: force-unauthorized
params.description: Sets the port state as unauthorized.

usageSummary: The following example shows how to retain the interface template if the link is down or the device is disconnected: Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 15. x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 2034 Security access-session (template) Device# configure terminal Device(config)# template user-template1 Device(config-template)# access-session interface-template sticky Device(config-template)# end Related Commands Command Description authentication (template) Configures authentication manager settings for interface templates.

name: access-session tunnel vlan
detail: Configure an access session for a VLAN tunnel.

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: Specifies the tunnel VLAN ID. The range is from 1 to 4096.

usageSummary: Before you use this command, you must configure a VLAN using the vlan command.  You can use the show access-session command to verify access session settings.

name: activate
detail: Activate a control policy or service template on a subscriber session.
syntax: action-number activate {policy type control subscriber control-policy-name | service-template template-name [aaa-list list-name] [precedence number] [replace-all]}

params.name: action-number
params.description: Action identifier. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule. policy type control subscriber Specifies the name of the control policy to apply to a session, as defined

params.name: control-policy-name
params.description: by the policy-map type control subscriber command.

params.name: service-template
params.description: Specifies the name of the service template to apply to a session. This

params.name: template-name
params.description: template can be defined locally with the service-template command or downloaded from an authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) server.

params.name: aaa-list list-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of the AAA method list that identifies the AAA server from which to download the service template. If this is not specified, the template must be locally defined.

params.name: precedence number
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the priority level of the service template. Range: 1 to 254, where 1 is the highest priority and 254 is the lowest.

params.name: replace-all
params.description: (Optional) Replaces all existing authorization data and services with new data and services.

usageSummary: The activate command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before actions are executed.

name: authenticate using
detail: Initiate the authentication of a subscriber session using the specified method.
syntax: action-number authenticate using {dot1x | mab | webauth}[aaa {authc-list authc-list-name | authz-list authz-list-name}] [merge] [parameter-map parameter-map-name] [priority priority-number] [replace | replace-all] [retries number {retry-time seconds}]

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

params.name: dot1x
params.description: Specifies the IEEE 802.1X authentication method.

params.name: mab
params.description: Specifies the MAC authentication bypass (MAB) method.

params.name: webauth
params.description: Specifies the web authentication method.

params.name: aaa
params.description: (Optional) Indicates that authentication is performed using an authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) method list.

params.name: authc-list authc-list-name
params.description: Specifies the name of AAA method list to use for authentication requests.

params.name: authz-list authz-list-name
params.description: Specifies the name of AAA method list to use for authorization requests.

params.name: merge
params.description: (Optional) Merges the new data and services into the existing authorization data and services.

params.name: parameter-map
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of a parameter map to use for web

params.name: parameter-map-name
params.description: authentication, as defined by the parameter map type webauth command.

params.name: priority priority-number
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the priority of the selected authentication method. Allows a higher priority method to interrupt an authentication in progress with a lower priority method. Range: 1 to 254, where 1 is the highest priority and 254 is the lowest. The default priority order is dot1x, mab, then webauth.

params.name: replace
params.description: (Optional) Replace existing authorization data with the new authorization data.

params.name: replace-all
params.description: (Optional) Replace all existing authorization data and services with the new data and services. This is the default behavior.

params.name: retries number
params.description: (Optional) Number of times to retry an authentication method if the initial attempt fails. Range: 1 to 5. Default: 2.

params.name: retry-time seconds
params.description: Number of seconds between authentication attempts. Range: 0 to 65535. Default: 30.

usageSummary: The authenticate using command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: authentication-restart
detail: Restart the authentication process after an authentication or authorization failure.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Number of seconds to wait before restarting the authentication process after a failure occurs. Range: 1 to 65535.

usageSummary: The authentication-restart command configures an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: authentication display
detail: Set the configuration display mode for Identity-Based Networking Services.

params.name: legacy
params.description: Displays the configuration using the legacy authentication manager style. This is the default mode. new-style Displays the configuration using the Cisco common classification policy language (C3PL) style that supports Identity-Based Networking Services.

usageSummary: Use the authentication display command to enable the configuration display mode that supports Identity-Based Networking Services.  This command allows you to switch between the two different display modes until you enter a configuration for Identity-Based Networking Services.  After you enter a configuration that is specific to Identity-Based Networking Services, this command is disabled and becomes unavailable.

name: authorize
detail: Initiate the authorization of a subscriber session.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The authorize command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions will be executed.

name: banner
detail: Display a banner on the web-authentication login web page.

params.name: text banner-text
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a text string to use as the banner. You must enter a delimiting character before and after the banner text. The delimiting character can be any character of your choice, such as “c” or “@.”

usageSummary: The banner command allows you to configure one of three possible scenarios: • The banner command without any keyword or argument—Displays the default banner using the name of the device: “Cisco Systems, <device’s hostname> Authentication. ” • The banner command with the file filename keyword-argument pair—Displays the banner from the custom HTML file you supply.  The custom HTML file must be stored in the disk or flash of the device.

name: class-map type control subscriber
detail: Create a control class, which defines the conditions under which the actions of a control policy are executed.
syntax: class-map type control subscriber {match-all | match-any | match-none} control-class-name
noForm: no class-map type control subscriber {match-all | match-any | match-none} control-class-name

params.name: match-all
params.description: Specifies that all conditions in the control class must evaluate true.

params.name: match-any
params.description: Specifies that at least one of the conditions in the control class must evaluate true.

params.name: match-none
params.description: Specifies that all conditions in the control class must evaluate false. control-class-name Name of the control class.

usageSummary: A control class defines the conditions that must be met for the actions in a control policy to be executed.  A control class can contain multiple conditions.  Use the match-any, match-all, or match-none keywords to specify which, if any, of the conditions the subscriber session must match for the actions to be executed.

name: class
detail: Associate a control class with one or more actions in a control policy.

params.name: priority-number
params.description: Relative priority of the control class within the policy rule. This priority determines the order in which control policies are applied to a session. Range: 1 to 254, where 1 is the highest priority and 254 is the lowest. control-class-name Name of a previously configured control class as defined by the class-map type control subscriber command.

params.name: always
params.description: Creates a default control class that always evaluates true.

params.name: do-all
params.description: (Optional) Executes all actions.

params.name: do-until-failure
params.description: (Optional) Executes actions, in order, until one of the actions fails. This is the default behavior.

params.name: do-until-success
params.description: (Optional) Executes actions, in order, until one of the actions is successful.

usageSummary: The class command associates the conditions in a control class with one or more actions in a control policy.  A control class defines the conditions that must be met before a set of actions are executed.  The association of a control class and a set of actions is called a control policy rule.

name: clear-authenticated-data-hosts-on-port
detail: Clear authenticated data hosts on a port after an authentication failure.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The clear-authenticated-data-hosts-on-port command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: clear-session
detail: Clear an active subscriber session.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The clear-session command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: consent email
detail: Request a user’s e-mail address on the consent login web page.
usageSummary: Use the consent email command to display a text box on the consent login page prompting the user to enter his or her e-mail address for identification.  The device sends this e-mail address to the authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) server instead of sending the client’s MAC address.  The consent feature allows you to provide temporary Internet and corporate access to end users through their wired and wireless networks by presenting a consent web page.

name: custom-page
detail: Display custom web pages during web authentication login.
noForm: no custom-page {failure | login [expired] | success} device location:filename

params.name: failure
params.description: Displays the custom web page if the login fails.

params.name: login
params.description: Displays the custom web page during login.

params.name: expired
params.description: (Optional) Displays the custom web page if the login expires.

params.name: success
params.description: Displays the custom web page when the login is successful. location :filename Location and name of the locally stored HTML file to use in place of the default HTML file for the specified condition.

usageSummary: Use the custom-page command to display custom web pages during web authentication login.  To enable custom web pages: • You must specify all four custom HTML files.  If fewer than four files are specified, the internal default HTML pages are used.

name: deactivate
detail: Deactivate a control policy or service template on a subscriber session.
syntax: action-number deactivate {policy type control subscriber control-policy-name | service-template template-name}

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

params.name: policy type control subscriber
params.description: Specifies the name of the control policy to deactivate on the session,

params.name: control-policy-name
params.description: as defined by the policy-map type control subscriber command.

params.name: service-template template-name
params.description: Specifies the name of the service template to deactivate on the session, as defined by the service-template command.

usageSummary: The deactivate command defines an action in a control policy.  This command uninstalls all control policies and policy attributes that have been applied on the session.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.

name: debug access-session
detail: Display debugging information about Session Aware Networking sessions.
syntax: debug access-session [feature feature-name] {all | detail | errors | events | sync}
noForm: no debug access-session [feature feature-name] {all | detail | errors | events | sync}

params.name: all
params.description: Displays all debugging information for Session Aware Networking.

params.name: detail
params.description: Displays detailed debugging information.

params.name: errors
params.description: Displays debugging information about errors.

params.name: events
params.description: Displays debugging information about events.

params.name: sync
params.description: Displays debugging information about stateful switchovers (SSOs) or In Service Software Upgrades (ISSUs).

usageSummary: Use the debug access-session command to troubleshoot Session Aware Networking sessions.  Related Commands Command Description debug Displays debugging information about the Authentication Manager.  authentication debug dot1x Displays 802.

name: description
detail: Add a description to a service template.

params.name: description
params.description: Description of the service template.

usageSummary: Use the description command to provide additional information about the service template when you display the service template configuration.

name: dot1x pae
detail: Set the Port Access Entity (PAE) type using an interface template.

params.name: supplicant
params.description: (Optional) The interface acts only as a supplicant and will not respond to messages that are meant for an authenticator.

params.name: authenticator
params.description: (Optional) The interface acts only as an authenticator and will not respond to any messages meant for a supplicant.

name: err-disable
detail: Disable a port after a security violation occurs.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The err-disable command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the policy can execute the actions.

name: event
detail: Specify the type of event that triggers actions in a control policy if conditions are met.

params.name: match-all
params.description: (Optional) Evaluates all control classes. This is the default behavior. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.15.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 2064 Security event match-first (Optional) Evaluates only the first control class.

usageSummary: The event command configures an event condition in a control policy.  After the specified event occurs, the system evaluates the control classes.  Control classes specify the conditions that must be met to execute the actions in the control policy.

name: guest-lan
detail: Configure the wireless guest LAN.

params.name: profile-name
params.description: Specifies the wireless guest profile name.

params.name: lan-id
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the guest LAN identifier. The range is from 1 to 5.

usageSummary: Use the guest-lan command to specify a wireless guest profile.  This wireless guest profile is used in the tunnel type capwap command to configure a a CAPWAP tunnel within a service template and configure wired guest access for guest users of an enterprise network.

name: inactivity-timer
detail: Enable an inactivity timeout for subscriber sessions.

params.name: probe
params.description: (Optional) Enables address resolution protocol (ARP) probes. These probes are sent before terminating the session.

usageSummary: Use the inactivity-timer command to set the maximum amount of time that a subscriber session can exist with no activity or data from the end client.  If this timer expires before there is any activity or data, the session is cleared.  The probe keyword enables ARP probes.

name: ip dhcp snooping trust
detail: Configure an interface or template as trusted for DHCP snooping.

name: Keepalive
detail: Enable keepalive timer for interface templates.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Sets the keepalive timer in seconds. The range is from 0 to 32767. Default is 10.

name: key-wrap enable
detail: Enable Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) key wrap on a RADIUS server.
usageSummary: Use the key-wrap enable command to enable AES key-wrap functionality.  The AES key-wrap feature makes the shared secret between the controller and the RADIUS server more secure.  AES key wrap is designed for Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) customers and requires a key-wrap compliant RADIUS authentication server.

name: linksec policy
detail: Set a data link layer security policy.

params.name: must-not-secure
params.description: Specifies that the session must not be secured with Media Access Control Security (MACsec) standard.

params.name: must-secure
params.description: Specifies that the device port must be authorized only if a secure MACsec session is established.

params.name: should-secure
params.description: Specifies that the link security policy has optionally secured sessions. If an attempt to establish a MACsec session fails, an authorization failure message is not sent.

usageSummary: Configure the link layer security policy within a service template and its associated policy action.

name: mac-delimiter
detail: Specify the MAC delimiter for RADIUS compatibility mode.

params.name: colon
params.description: Sets the delimiter to a colon, in the format xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx.

params.name: hyphen
params.description: Sets the delimiter to a hyphen (-), in the format xx-xx-xx-xx-xx-xx.

params.name: none
params.description: Sets the delimiter to none, in the format xxxxxxxxxxxx. This is the default value. single-hyphen Sets the delimiter to a single hyphen, in the format xxxxxx-xxxxxx.

usageSummary: Use the mac-delimiter command to set the delimiter that is used in MAC addresses that are sent to the RADIUS authentication server.

name: match activated-service-template
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on the service template activated on a session.
syntax: match activated-service-template template-name no-match activated-service-template template-name

params.name: template-name
params.description: Name of a configured service template as defined by the service-template command.

usageSummary: The match activated-service-template command configures a match condition in a control class based on the service template applied to a session.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true for the actions of the control policy to be executed.

name: match authorization-failure
detail: Create a condition that returns true, based on the type of authorization failure of a session.

params.name: linksec-failed
params.description: Specifies that the data link security has failed.

params.name: tunnel-return
params.description: Specifies that the Converged Guest Access (CGA) tunnel authorization has failed.

usageSummary: The match authorization-failed command configures a match condition in a control class based on the type of authorization failure that is configured for a session.  Authorization failure can be either a data link layer security failure or a domain change failure.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, that are evaluated as either true or false.

name: match authorization-status
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on a session’s authorization status.
syntax: match authorization-status {authorized | unauthorized} no-match authorization-status {authorized | unauthorized}

params.name: authorized
params.description: Specifies that the subscriber has been authenticated. unauthorized Specifies that the subscriber has not been authenticated.

usageSummary: The match authorization-status command configures a match condition in a control class based on the session’s authorization status.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match authorizing-method-priority
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on the priority of the authorization method that resulted in authorization.
syntax: match authorizing-method-priority {eq | gt | lt} priority-value no-match authorizing-method-priority {eq | gt | lt} priority-value
noForm: no {match | no-match} authorizing-method-priority {eq | gt | lt} priority-value

params.name: eq
params.description: Specifies that the current priority value is equal to priority-value.

params.name: gt
params.description: Specifies that the current priority value is greater than priority-value. Note The higher the number, the lower the priority.

params.name: lt
params.description: Specifies that the current priority value is less than priority-value. Note The lower the number, the higher the priority. priority-value Priority value to match. Range: 1 to 254, where 1 is the highest priority and 254 is the lowest.

usageSummary: The match authorizing-method-priority command configures a match condition in a control class based on the priority of the authentication method that resulted in authorization.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match client-type
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on an event’s device type.
syntax: match client-type {data | switch | video | voice} no-match client-type {data | switch | video | voice}
noForm: no {match | no-match} client-type {data | switch | video | voice}

params.name: data
params.description: Specifies a data device. switch Specifies a switch device.

params.name: video
params.description: Specifies a video device.

params.name: voice
params.description: Specifies a voice device.

usageSummary: The match client-type command configures a match condition in a control class based on an event’s device type.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match current-method-priority
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on the priority of the current authentication method.
syntax: match current-method-priority {eq | gt | lt} priority-value no-match current-method-priority {eq | gt | lt} priority-value

params.name: eq
params.description: Specifies that the current priority value is equal to priority-value.

params.name: gt
params.description: Specifies that the current priority value is greater than priority-value. The higher the value, the lower the priority. Note The higher the number, the lower the priority.

params.name: lt
params.description: Specifies that the current priority value is less than priority-value. The lower the value, the higher the priority. Note The lower the number, the higher the priority. priority-value Priority value to match. Range: 1 to 254, where 1 is the highest priority and 254 is the lowest.

usageSummary: The match current-method-priority command configures a match condition in a control class based on the priority of the authentication method.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match ip-address
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on an event’s source IPv4 address.
syntax: match ip-address ip-address no-match ip-address ip-address

params.name: ip-address
params.description: IPv4 address to match.

usageSummary: The match ip-address command configures a match condition in a control class based on an event’s IP address.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match ipv6-address
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on an event’s source IPv6 address.
syntax: match ipv6-address ipv6-address subnet-mask no-match ipv6-address ipv6-address subnet-mask
usageSummary: The match ipv6-address command configures a match condition in a control class based on the subscriber’s IPv6 address.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match mac-address
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on an event’s MAC address.
syntax: match mac-address mac-address no-match mac-address mac-address

params.name: mac-address
params.description: MAC address to match.

usageSummary: The match mac-address command configures a match condition in a control class based on an event’s MAC address.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match method
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on the authentication method of an event.
syntax: match method {dot1x | mab | webauth} no-match method {dot1x | mab | webauth}

params.name: dot1x
params.description: Specifies the IEEE 802.1X authentication method.

params.name: mab
params.description: Specifies the MAC authentication bypass (MAB) method. webauth Specifies the web authentication method.

usageSummary: The match method command configures a match condition in a control class based on the authentication method.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match port-type
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on an event’s interface type.
syntax: match port-type {l2-port | l3-port | dot11-port} no-match port-type {l2-port | l3-port | dot11-port}

params.name: l2-port
params.description: Specifies the Layer 2 interface.

params.name: l3-port
params.description: Specifies the Layer 3 interface.

usageSummary: The match port-type command configures a match condition in a control class based on the interface type.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match result-type
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on the specified authentication result.
syntax: match result-type [method {dot1x | mab | webauth}] result-type no-match result-type [method {dot1x | mab | webauth}] result-type
noForm: no {match | no-match} result-type [method {dot1x | mab | webauth}] result-type

params.name: method
params.description: (Optional) Matches results for the specified authentication method only. If you do not specify a method, the policy matches the method associated with the current event.

params.name: dot1x
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the IEEE 802.1X authentication method.

params.name: mab
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the MAC authentication bypass (MAB) method. webauth (Optional) Specifies the web authentication method. result-type Type of authentication result. Valid keywords for result-type are: • aaa-timeout—authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) server timed out. • agent-not-found— The agent for the authentication method was not detected. • authoritative—Authorization failed. • method-timeout—The authentication method timed out. • none—No result. • success—Authentication was successful.

usageSummary: The match result-type command configures a match condition in a control class based on the result of the authentication request.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match service-template
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on an event’s service template.
syntax: match service-template template-name no-match service-template template-name

params.name: template-name
params.description: Name of a configured service template as defined by the service-template command.

usageSummary: The match service-template command configures a match condition in a control class based on an event’s service template.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match tag
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on the tag associated with an event.
syntax: match tag tag-name no-match tag tag-name

params.name: tag-name
params.description: Tag name, as defined by the tag command in a service template.

usageSummary: The match tag command configures a match condition in a control class based on an event’s tag.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: match username
detail: Create a condition that evaluates true based on an event’s username.
syntax: match username username no-match username username

params.name: username
params.description: Username.

usageSummary: The match username command configures a match condition in a control class based on the username.  A control class can contain multiple conditions, each of which will evaluate as either true or false.  The control class defines whether all, any, or none of the conditions must evaluate true to execute the actions of the control policy.

name: max-http-conns
detail: Limit the number of HTTP connections for each web authentication client.

params.name: number
params.description: Maximum number of concurrent HTTP client connections allowed. Range: 1 to 200. Default: 30.

usageSummary: Use the max-http-conns command to set the maximum number of HTTP connections allowed for each web authentication client.  If a new value is configured that is less than the previously configured value while the current number of connections exceeds the new maximum value, the HTTP server will not abort any of the current connections.  However, the server will not accept new connections until the current number of connections falls below the new configured value.

name: parameter-map type webauth
detail: Define a parameter map for web authentication.

params.name: global
params.description: Defines global parameters for web authentication.

usageSummary: Use the parameter-map type webauth command to define a parameter map for web authentication.  A parameter map allows you to specify parameters that control the behavior of actions configured under a policy map with the authenticate using webauth command.  A global parameter map contains system-wide parameters.

name: pause reauthentication
detail: Pause the reauthentication process after an authentication failure.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The pause reauthentication command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: peer neighbor-route
detail: Create neighbor route to a peer.
syntax: peer neighbor-route This command has no arguments or keywords.

name: policy-map type control subscriber
detail: Define a control policy for subscriber sessions.
noForm: no policy-map type control subscriber control-policy-name

params.name: control-policy-name
params.description: Name of the control policy.

usageSummary: Control policies define the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  A control policy consists of one or more control policy rules.  A control policy rule associates a control class with one or more actions.

name: protect
detail: Silently drop violating packets after a security violation on a port.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The protect command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: redirect
detail: Redirect users to a particular URL during web authentication login.
syntax: redirect { {for-login | on-failure | on-success} url | portal {ipv4 ipv4-address | ipv6 ipv6-address}}
noForm: no redirect {for-login | on-failure | on-success | portal {ipv4 | ipv6}}

params.name: for-login
params.description: Sends users to this URL for login.

params.name: on-failure
params.description: Sends users to this URL if the login fails.

params.name: on-success
params.description: Sends users to this URL if the login is successful.

params.name: url
params.description: Valid URL.

params.name: portal
params.description: Sends users to this external web server to access the customized login web pages.

params.name: ipv4
params.description: Specifies the IPv4 address of the portal. ipv4-address

params.name: ipv6
params.description: Specifies the IPv6 address of the portal. ipv6-address

usageSummary: Use the redirect command to redirect users to custom web pages stored on an external server during the authentication process.  The device redirects the client to the specified portal IP address after it intercepts the initial HTTP request.  The device also intercepts the login form sent by the client so it can extract the username and password and authenticates the user.

name: redirect url
detail: Redirect clients to a particular URL.
noForm: no redirect url url [match access-list-name [one-time-redirect | redirect-on-no-match]]

params.name: url
params.description: Valid URL. match access-list-name (Optional) Specifies the name of an access control list to match.

params.name: one-time-redirect
params.description: (Optional) Redirects traffic matching the access list only once. redirect-on-no-match (Optional) Redirects traffic not matching the access list.

usageSummary: Use the redirect url command to redirect clients to a particular URL when the service template is activated on a subscriber session.

name: replace
detail: Clear the existing session and create a new session after a security violation on a port.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The replace command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: restrict
detail: Drop violating packets and generate a syslog message after a security violation on a port.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The restrict command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: resume reauthentication
detail: Resume the reauthentication process after an authentication failure.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The resume reauthentication command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: service-policy type control subscriber
detail: Apply a control policy to an interface.
noForm: no service-policy type control subscriber control-policy-name
usageSummary: A control policy is activated by applying it to one or more interfaces.  Control policies apply to all sessions hosted on the interface.  Only one control policy may be applied to a given interface.

name: service-template
detail: Define a template that contains a set of service policy attributes to apply to subscriber sessions.

params.name: template-name
params.description: Alphanumeric name that identifies the service template.

usageSummary: Use the service-template command to group attributes that can be applied to subscriber sessions that share the same characteristics.  More than one template can be defined but only one template can be associated with a single subscriber session.

name: set-timer
detail: Start a named policy timer for a subscriber session.

params.name: timer-name
params.description: Name of the policy timer, up to 15 characters. This is an arbitrary name defined for this action.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Timer interval, in seconds. Range: 1 to 65535.

usageSummary: The set-timer command configures an action in a control policy.  This command starts the named policy timer.  After the named timer expires, the system generates the timer-expiry event.

name: show access-session
detail: Display information about Session Aware Networking sessions.
syntax: show access-session [[database] [handle handle-number | [method method] [interface interface-type interface-number] | mac mac-address | session-id session-id] | history [min-uptime seconds] | registrations | statistics] [details]

params.name: database
params.description: (Optional) Displays session data stored in the session database. This allows you to see information like the VLAN ID which is not cached internally. A warning message displays if data stored in the session database does not match the internally cached data.

params.name: handle handle-number
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the specified context handle number. Range: 1 to 4294967295.

params.name: method method
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about subscriber sessions using one of the following authentication methods: • dot1x—IEEE 802.1X authentication method. • mab—MAC authentication bypass (MAB) method. • webauth—Web authentication method. If you specify a method, you can also specify an interface.

params.name: interface interface-type
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about subscriber sessions that match

params.name: interface-number
params.description: the specified client interface type. To display the valid keywords and arguments for interfaces, use the question mark (?) online help function.

params.name: mac mac-address
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about subscriber sessions with the specified client MAC address.

params.name: session-id session-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about subscriber sessions with the specified client session identifier.

params.name: history
params.description: (Optional) Displays session history.

params.name: min-uptime seconds
params.description: (Optional) Displays session history for sessions that have been up for the specified number of seconds. Range: 1 to 4294967295.

params.name: registrations
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about all registered session manager clients including the registered authentication methods.

params.name: statistics
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about authentication session statistics.

params.name: details
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about each session instead of displaying a single-line summary. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.15.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 2114 Security show access-session

usageSummary: If you enter the show access-session command without any keywords or arguments, the information displays for all sessions on the switch.  When you specify an identifier, information displays for only those sessions that match the identifier.

name: show class-map type control subscriber
detail: Display information about session aware networking control classes.
syntax: show class-map type control subscriber {all | name control-class-name}

params.name: all
params.description: Displays output for all control classes. name control-class-name Displays output for the named control class.

usageSummary: Control policies define the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  Use the show class-map type control subscriber command to display information about configured control classes, including the number of times each match condition within the class has been executed.

name: show policy-map type control subscriber
detail: Display information about session aware networking control policies.
syntax: show policy-map type control subscriber {all | name control-policy-name}

params.name: all
params.description: Displays output for all control policies. name control-policy-name Displays output for the named control policy.

usageSummary: Control policies define the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  Use the show policy-map type control subscriber command to display information about configured control policies, including the number of times each policy-rule within the policy map has been executed.

name: show service-template
detail: Display information about configured service templates.

params.name: template-name
params.description: (Optional) Name of the service template.

usageSummary: Service templates define service policy attributes that can be applied to subscriber sessions.  Use the show service-template command to display information about configured service templates.  Using this command without the service-template argument displays a summary of all configured service templates.

name: source template
detail: Source the configurations from a template other than the configured template.

params.name: template-name
params.description: String that identifies the source template.

usageSummary: Use this command to source configurations from a template that is different than the configured template.

name: spanning tree portfast
detail: Enable PortFast mode where the interface is immediately put into the forwarding state upon linkup without waiting for the timer to expire using an interface template.

params.name: trunk
params.description: Enables PortFast on the interface in the trunk mode.

usageSummary: Use this command only with interfaces that connect to end stations; otherwise, an accidental topology loop could cause a data-packet loop and disrupt the device and network operation.  An interface with PortFast mode enabled is moved directly to the spanning-tree forwarding state when a linkup occurs, without waiting for the standard forward-time delay.

name: storm-control
detail: Enable broadcast, multicast, or unicast storm control on a port or to specify the action when a storm occurs on a port using an interface template.
syntax: storm-control { {broadcast | multicast | unicast} level [ bps | pps] rising-threshold [falling-threshold] | action {shutdown | trap}}
noForm: no storm-control { {broadcast | multicast | unicast} level | action {shutdown | trap}}

params.name: broadcast
params.description: Enables broadcast storm control on the port.

params.name: multicast
params.description: Enables multicast storm control on the port.

params.name: unicast
params.description: Enables unicast storm control on the port.

params.name: level rising-threshold
params.description: Defines the rising and falling suppression levels. falling-threshold • rising-threshold falling-threshold—Rising and falling suppression level as a percent of the total bandwidth (up to two decimal places). The valid values are from 0 to 100. When the value specified for a level is reached, the flooding of storm packets is blocked. • If you enter the level as a bits per second (bps) or packets per second (pps), the range is from 0 to 10000000000.

params.name: bps
params.description: Defines the rising and falling suppression levels in bits per second.

params.name: pps
params.description: Defines the rising and falling suppression levels in packets per second.

params.name: action
params.description: Specifies the action to take when a storm occurs on a port. The default action is to filter traffic.

params.name: shutdown
params.description: Disables the port during a storm.

params.name: trap
params.description: Sends a Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) trap.

usageSummary: Use the storm-control command to enable or disable broadcast, multicast, or unicast storm control on a port.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 15.

name: subscriber aging
detail: Enable an inactivity timer for subscriber sessions.

params.name: probe
params.description: Enables an address resolution protocol (ARP) probe.

usageSummary: Use the subscriber aging command to set the maximum amount of time that a subscriber session can exist with no activity or data from the end client.  If this timer expires before there is any activity or data, the session is cleared.  The following example shows how to set the inactivity timer to 60 seconds on Ten Gigabit Ethernet interface 1/0/2: interface TenGigabitEthernet 1/0/2 subscriber aging inactivity-timer 60 probe service-policy type control subscriber POLICY_1 Related Commands inactivity-timer Enables an inactivity timeout for subscriber sessions.

name: subscriber mac-filtering security-mode
detail: Specify the RADIUS compatibility mode for MAC filtering.
noForm: no subscriber mac-filtering security-mode {mac | none | shared-secret}

params.name: mac
params.description: Sends the MAC address as the password.

params.name: none
params.description: Does not send the password attribute. This is the default value. shared-secret Sends the shared-secret as the password.

usageSummary: Use the subscriber mac-filtering security-mode command to set the type of security used for MAC filtering in RADIUS compatibility mode.  The following example shows how to configure a server group with MAC filtering to send the MAC address as the password: aaa group server radius LAB_RAD key-wrap enable subscriber mac-filtering security-mode mac mac-delimiter colon Related Commands Command Description key-wrap enable Enables AES key wrap.  mac-delimiter Specifies the MAC delimiter for RADIUS compatibility mode.

name: subscriber aging
detail: Configure the inactivity timeout value of the subscriber.

params.name: probe
params.description: Sets Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) probe.

name: tag
detail: Associate a user-defined tag with a service template.

params.name: tag-name
params.description: Arbitrary text string assigned as the tag name.

usageSummary: Use the tag command to associate an identifier tag with a service template.  The tag is applied to a session when a control policy activates the service template on the session.  A set of policies can be associated with the tag and if the authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) server sends the same tag in response to the authentication response, the policies that are associated with the tag are applied on the host.

name: terminate
detail: Terminate an authentication method on a subscriber session.

params.name: dot1x
params.description: Specifies the IEEE 802.1X authentication method.

params.name: mab
params.description: Specifies the MAC authentication bypass (MAB) method.

params.name: webauth
params.description: Specifies the web authentication method.

usageSummary: The terminate command defines an action in a control policy.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.  The control class defines the conditions that must be met before the actions are executed.

name: timeout init-state min
detail: Set the initialize (Init) state timeout for web authentication sessions.
usageSummary: Use the timeout init-state min command to limit the number of minutes that a web authentication session can stay in the Init state.  A session remains in the Init state until the user enters his or her username and password credentials.  If the timer expires before the user enters his or her credentials, the session is cleared.

name: trust device
detail: Set a trust state for a device.

params.name: device-name
params.description: Name of the device to be assigned a trust state, which can be one of the following values: • cisco-phone • cts • ip-camera • media-player

usageSummary: Use this command to set the trust state of an end device.

name: tunnel type capwap
detail: Configure a Control And Provisioning of Wireless Access Points protocol (CAPWAP) tunnel in a service template.
noForm: no tunnel type capwap name tunnel-name

params.name: name
params.description: tunnel-name Specified the name of the CAPWAP tunnel.

usageSummary: Use this command to create a CAPWAP tunnel to enable wired guest access through a wireless port.  For wireless access, guests are directed through a Control And Provisioning of Wireless Access Points (CAPWAP) tunnel to the wireless controller in the DMZ (demilitarized zone) and are provided open or web-authenticated access from the wireless controller.  The following example shows how to configure a CAPWAP tunnel: Device(config)# service-template GUEST-TUNNEL Device(config-service-template)# tunnel type capwap name tunnel1 Related Commands Command Description service-template Defines a template that contains a set of service policy attributes to apply to subscriber sessions.

name: type
detail: Define the authentication methods supported by a parameter map.

params.name: consent
params.description: Specifies consent only. Allows default access without prompting users for their username and password credentials. Users instead get a choice of two radio buttons: accept or do not accept. For accounting purposes, the device passes the client’s MAC address to the authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) server.

params.name: webauth
params.description: Specifies web authentication only. Allows access based on the user's privileges. The device sends the username and password to the AAA server for authentication and accounting. This is the default value. webconsent Specifies both web authentication and consent.

usageSummary: Use the type command to specify the authentication method to which the parameters in the map apply.  A parameter map defines parameters that control the behavior of actions specified under a policy map.  This command is supported in named parameter maps only.

name: unauthorize
detail: Unauthorize a port and remove any access granted on the basis of previous authorization data.

params.name: action-number
params.description: Number of the action. Actions are executed sequentially within the policy rule.

usageSummary: The unauthorize command defines an action in a control policy.  This command removes any access that was granted based on previous authorization data, including the user profile and any activated service templates.  Control policies determine the actions taken in response to specified events and conditions.

name: virtual-ip
detail: Specify a virtual IP address for web authentication clients.

params.name: ipv4 ipv4-address
params.description: Specifies the IPv4 address to use as the virtual IP address.

params.name: ipv6 ipv6-address
params.description: Specifies the IPv6 address to use as the virtual IP address.

usageSummary: Use the virtual-ip command to specify the virtual IP address to use for web authentication clients.  If you use default or local custom pages, configuring a virtual IP address will cause a logout web page to be presented to clients after they have been successfully authenticated.  This allows users to logout by clicking a link in the logout page.

name: vlan
detail: Assign a VLAN to subscriber sessions.

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: VLAN identifier. Range: 1 to 4094.

usageSummary: Use the vlan command to assign a VLAN to sessions on which the service template is activated.

name: voice vlan
detail: Assign a voice VLAN to subscriber sessions.
usageSummary: Use the voice vlan command to assign a voice VLAN to sessions on which the service template is activated.

name: watch-list
detail: Enable a watch list of web authentication clients.
syntax: watch-list {add-item {ipv4 ipv4-address | ipv6 ipv6-address} | dynamic-expiry-timeout minutes | enabled} | enabled}
noForm: no watch-list {add-item {ipv4 ipv4-address | ipv6 ipv6-address} | dynamic-expiry-timeout minutes

params.name: add-item
params.description: Adds an IP address to the watch list.

params.name: ipv4 ipv4-address
params.description: Specifies the IPv4 address of a client to add to the watch list.

params.name: ipv6 ipv6-address
params.description: Specifies the IPv6 address of a client to add to the watch list. dynamic-expiry-timeout minutes Sets the duration of time, in minutes, that an entry remains in the watch list. Range: 0 to 2147483647. Default: 30. 0 (zero) keeps the entry in the list permanently.

params.name: enabled
params.description: Enables a watch list.

usageSummary: Use the watch-list command to monitor the connections of specific web authentication clients.  When you enable the watch list, web authentication dynamically adds clients to the watch list after either of the following events occurs: • The client exceeds the maximum number of login attempts allowed, as configured with the ip admission max-login-attempts command.  • The client exceeds the maximum number of open TCP sessions allowed, as configured with the max-http-conns command (default is 30).
